hero.question: Will Israel strike 3 countries in 2026?
hero.prob_take: The crowd's pricing a 17.2% shot at Israel striking 3+ countries in 2026, which feels like it's extrapolating from recent escalation without accounting for how quickly regional conflicts cool when the immediate trigger is removed. The +6.5 point spike suggests new shorts covering or fresh Iran/Hezbollah concerns, but you're getting paid well to fade panic: historical precedent says multi-front strikes cluster around acute crises (October 7 aftermath, recent retaliatory cycles), not calendar years where tensions naturally diffuse.

daily_take.headline: Israel's Three-Country Strike Bet Just Jumped 6.5 Points. Here's Why That Matters.
daily_take.deck: The 17.2% odds on Israel striking 3+ countries in 2026 spiked hard in 24 hours, signaling real money is pricing in escalation risk. But compare this to the actual geopolitical runway and you'll spot the gap. Smart bettors should ask whether recent rhetoric justifies a 23.7% year-ago baseline or if this is panic pricing begging to be faded.
